journal_entry.description: Deposit received for moving services to be performed in one month
journal_entry.rationale: Under IFRS 15, revenue is recognized when performance obligations are satisfied. Atlas has received $5,000 cash but has not yet performed the moving services. The deposit creates a liability (unearned revenue/deferred revenue) because Atlas has an obligation to either perform the service or refund the money. The asset increases (cash in chequing account) and liability increases (unearned revenue). No revenue is recognized at this point because the performance obligation (providing moving services) will be satisfied in one month. The transaction is not taxable at deposit stage; HST/GST will be applicable when services are rendered and revenue is recognized.

output.reason: Atlas receives $5,000 cash deposit via bank transfer. This increases the chequing account (bank/cash asset). Debit: 1 asset increase. The credit side (not classified here) would be a liability increase for unearned revenue/customer deposit, as the moving services have not yet been performed.

debit_classifier.reason: Atlas receives $5,000 cash deposit via bank transfer. This increases the chequing account (bank/cash asset). Debit: 1 asset increase. The credit side (not classified here) would be a liability increase for unearned revenue/customer deposit, as the moving services have not yet been performed.

output.reason: Upon receiving the $5,000 deposit, Atlas has two credit-side effects: (1) Cash/chequing account increases = asset increase, which is NOT a credit-side event (assets are debited when increased). Actually, the deposit creates a liability because Atlas owes the customer the moving service in the future. The $5,000 received creates an unearned revenue/customer deposit liability = liability increase (1 line). No revenue is recognized yet because the service has not been performed under IFRS revenue recognition principles.

credit_classifier.reason: Upon receiving the $5,000 deposit, Atlas has two credit-side effects: (1) Cash/chequing account increases = asset increase, which is NOT a credit-side event (assets are debited when increased). Actually, the deposit creates a liability because Atlas owes the customer the moving service in the future. The $5,000 received creates an unearned revenue/customer deposit liability = liability increase (1 line). No revenue is recognized yet because the service has not been performed under IFRS revenue recognition principles.

output.reason: Entry correctly records deposit for future services. Dr Chequing Account $5,000 (asset increase), Cr Unearned Revenue $5,000 (liability increase). Balance verified. IFRS 15 treatment correct: no revenue recognized until performance obligation satisfied. Deposit transactions are not taxable events; tax applies when services are rendered. Accounts, amounts, and directionality all correct.
